summary: This week in AI news, significant investments and partnerships were announced, including ServiceNow's $40 million investment in BusinessNext and AMD's commitment of up to $5 billion to Anthropic. Google's cloud business is thriving, driven by the adoption of its AI and infrastructure services. Meanwhile, OpenAI's AI spending spree has raised eyebrows, with plans to spend the equivalent of Sweden's GDP on infrastructure through 2030.
insight: The increasing investment in AI infrastructure and services suggests a growing recognition of the importance of AI in driving business growth and innovation, with companies like Google, NVIDIA, and OpenAI leading the charge.
highlights: ServiceNow invests $40 million in BusinessNext to expand its AI-powered banking software globally.
Google's cloud business thrives, driven by the adoption of its AI and infrastructure services.
OpenAI's AI spending spree raises eyebrows, with plans to spend the equivalent of Sweden's GDP on infrastructure through 2030.

articles.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
articles.description: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

articles.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
articles.description: ServiceNow's investment gives BusinessNext a strategic partner to expand its AI-powered banking software globally.

articles.title: OpenAI’s AI spending spree has ballooned to $750B
articles.description: OpenAI will spend the equivalent of Sweden's GDP on infrastructure through 2030.

articles.title: NVIDIA AI Supercomputer Comes Online at Naval Postgraduate School

articles.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face

articles.title: OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
articles.description: "This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents," Hugging Face CEO says.

articles.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
articles.description: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

articles.title: Hyundai claims humanoid robot plan is not part of talks with striking workers
articles.description: Union previously warned automaker that any robot deployment must be negotiated.

articles.title: NVIDIA Open Sources First GPU-Accelerated Medical Physics Simulation Framework

articles.title: Building AI infrastructure with the Effingham County community
articles.description: OpenAI announces Project Camellia in Effingham County, Georgia, with commitments to responsible energy, community investment, jobs, and access to Codex.

articles.title: Introducing OpenAI Presence
articles.description: Introducing OpenAI Presence, a proven enterprise AI agent platform that helps organizations deploy trusted voice and chat agents for customer and internal workflows.

articles.title: Google announces Gemini 3.6 Flash and cybersecurity AI, teases 3.5 Pro and Gemini 4
articles.description: There are new 3.6 and 3.5 models today, but Google is already training Gemini 4.
